articles.title: School timings changed in Odisha; morning classes begin as heatwave intensifies - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Morning school timings came into effect across Odisha from Saturday, as the state government rolled out the revised schedule in response to the rising heat and prevailing heatwave conditions.
As per the directive issued by the School and Mass Education Department, schools will now function from 6:30 AM to 10:30 AM. The early morning schedule will continue until the summer vacation to ensure the safety and well-being of students amid soaring temperatures.
The department had earlier informed all district collectors regarding the implementation of morning classes across the state.
Alongside the change in timings, schools have been directed to make adequate arrangements for drinking water. Tube wells on school campuses will be repaired to ensure an uninterrupted supply of potable water.
To prevent dehydration among students, a ‘water bell’ system will be introduced, prompting children to drink water at regular intervals. Schools have also been instructed to maintain a sufficient stock of Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) for emergency use.

In view of the heatwave, outdoor activities during school hours have been restricted. Parents, on the other hand, have been advised to ensure that their children carry water bottles to school.
The decision aims to minimize students’ exposure to extreme heat as temperatures continue to climb across Odisha.
